rulings.Case Title: NORTH POINT HOLDINGS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. PAUL F UTRECHT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Paul Utrecht, Zacks & Utecht, P.C. F/K/A Zacks, Utrecht & Leadbetter'S Motion For Summary Judgment.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 2, 2010, line 6, Defendant Paul Utrecht, Zacks & Utecht, P.C. F/K/A Zacks, Utrecht & Leadbetter's Motion For Summary Judgment. GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING CAUSATION AND DAMAGES. THE PETITION WAS NOT PREMATURE AND PUBLIC RESOURCES CODE SECTION 21168 APPLIES. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF WOULD NOT HAVE PREVAILED IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION NORTH POINT HOLDINGS, LLC V. CCSF, ET.AL. CPF-07-507187, WHICH PROVIDES SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS' DECISION THAT A FAIR ARGUMENT OF SIGNIFICANT ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT EXISTS. THE COURT MAY NOT EXERCISE ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT AND REASONABLE DOUBTS MUST BE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE FINDINGS AND DECISION. THE BOARD FOUND THAT UNDER CEQA GUIDELINES THE PROJECT DID NOT QUALIFY FOR A CATEGORICAL EXEMPTION BECAUSE THE DEMOLITION MAY CAUSE A SUBSTANTIAL ADVERSE CHANGE IN THE SIGNIFICANCE OF A HISTORICAL RESOURCE, AND PROVIDED 3 REASONS FOR ITS DECISION, RELYING ON THE REPORT AND TESTIMONY OF BUTLER AND THE LETTER OF RICHARDSON. (AR BOS 00002-5.) EVEN IF THE RICHARDSON LETTER IS EXCLUDED, SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE BOARD'S DECISION CLEARLY ESTABLISHING THAT THE BOARD DID NOT ABUSE ITS DISCRETION AND A WRIT WOULD NOT HAVE ISSUED. AS A MATTER OF LAW, PLAINTIFF WOULD NOT HAVE PREVAILED IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION AND DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATION OF PLAINTIFF WOULD NOT HAVE BEEN THE CAUSE OF DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF. REQUEST TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT IS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AND THIS MOTION IS NOT DECIDED ON THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE PLEADING ALONE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EVANS NATIONAL LEASING, INC. VS. KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 2, 2010, line 7, PLAINTIFF EVANS NATIONAL LEASING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Evan¿s motion for summary judgment is continued until January 4, 2011. Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief not exceeding 5 pages by December 9, 2010 addressing the following questions: Assuming Defendants have introduced evidence sufficient to create a triable issue of fact as to whether ILS committed fraud in connection with the lease, what is the effect of paragraph 18 of the lease agreement, waiving defenses against an assignee, under Sections 9403 and 3305 of the California Commercial code? Is the evidence put forth in paragraph 7 of Kendra Tomcik¿s declaration evidence of fraud in the inducement, fraud in the execution, both or neither by an agent of ILS? Does it make a difference for purposes of the wavier of defenses against an assignee clause under Sections 9403 and 3305 of the California commercial code? Defendants shall respond, in a brief no longer than 5 pages, to Plaintiffs supplemental brief by December 16, 2010. Plaintiff's supplemental reply, of no more than 5 pages in length, is due December 23, 2010. Courtesy copies to Department 302. Briefs must be served and received by opposing party on the day filed. =(302/CWW)
